title: luzula campestris f. vulgaris
intro: Luzula campestris f. vulgaris, also known as Woodrush, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Juncaceae, characterized by its grass-like leaves and purple or brown flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of Woodrush include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune system function. Woodrush is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in salves and topical creams for skin issues and wound healing.
